candidates.comparison: async TP compiled (fuse_gemm_comms=True, VLLM_COMPILE) vs regular TP (no compilation)
candidates.oracle: exact dict equality (compare_two_settings -> assert ref_result == compare_result)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality on generated text, finish_reason, and usage via compare_two_settings/_test_completion. Text equality is a weak oracle when comparing across different compilation strategies.
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — fuse_gemm_comms=True changes GEMM+allreduce fusion and kernel selection relative to the unfused TP baseline. Different reduction orders and fused kernels can produce different FP accumulation, which can flip greedy token selection when logit differences are small.
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity. Not Strong #3: different compile strategies (fuse_gemm_comms pass) vs baseline. Not Strong #5: async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. No strong contract clause covers compiled-with-fusion vs non-compiled exact equality.
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)
